q1.explanation: Binary heap construction using Floyd's heapify algorithm runs in O(n) — each node is sifted down starting from the last internal node, and total work is sum of heights of all nodes = O(n).

q2.explanation: In the twin paradox, the traveling twin undergoes acceleration to turn around, making their reference frame non-inertial. Their worldline is shorter in proper time than the stay-at-home twin's, so they age less.

q3.explanation: Two consistent solutions: (A=knight, B=knave, C=knave) and (A=knave, B=knight, C=knave). In both, C is a knave. That's the only invariant.

q4.explanation: All pianists are musicians (2), all musicians are creative (1), no mathematician is creative (3). Therefore no pianist can be a mathematician.

q7.description: Implement length_of_lis(nums) that returns the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in O(n log n) time.

q8.description: Implement move_zeroes(nums) that moves all zeros to the end in-place while maintaining relative order of non-zero elements, using O(1) extra memory. Returns the modified list.

q9.explanation: P(at least 2 share a birthday) = 1 - (365/365)(364/365)...(343/365) ≈ 0.507. This is the birthday paradox — counterintuitively it exceeds 50% at just 23 people.

q10.explanation: bat + ball = 1.10, bat = ball + 1.00. Substituting: (ball + 1.00) + ball = 1.10 → 2·ball = 0.10 → ball = 0.05. The intuitive but wrong answer is 10¢.
